fields.summary: Recreate interceptor chain for different attemptId in the same node in AMRMProxy
Enable YARN RM scale out via federation using multiple RM's

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

issuetype.description: A new feature of the product, which has yet to be developed.
issuetype.name: New Feature

fields.description: In AMRMProxy, an interceptor chain is created per application attempt. But the pipeline mapping uses application Id as key. So when a different attempt comes in the same node, we need to recreate the interceptor chain for it, instead of using the existing one.

resolution.description: The problem is a duplicate of an existing issue.
resolution.name: Duplicate

fields.summary: Support multiple attempts on the node when AMRMProxy is enabled

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

This message was automatically generated.

[~botong] thanks for the patch, few questions:
 # while not very likely (required concurrent invocations of this object back-to-back) *chainWrapperBackup.getApplicationId()* could be null (as the wrapper is initialized after and not under the same synchronization), probably best check for that to avoid an NPE.
 # in case things "go wrong" do we have any safe behavior to put in a *finally* block to ensure we leave things tidy.

Hi [~curino], thanks for the review and potential bugs in this. This Jira turns out to be a duplicate for YARN-6234, where people are still debating whether we should create a new pipeline or reuse it for later attempts in the same node.
No problem, if you decide to go for this option, please consider the potential issues above.
Closing this as it's a duplicate of YARN-6234. [~botong]/[~curino], please continue the discussion there.

[~botong] thanks for the patch, few questions:
 # while not very likely (required concurrent invocations of this object back-to-back) *chainWrapperBackup.getApplicationId()* could be null (as the wrapper is initialized after and not under the same synchronization), probably best check for that to avoid an NPE.
 # in case things "go wrong" do we have any safe behavior to put in a *finally* block to ensure we leave things tidy.

Hi [~curino], thanks for the review and potential bugs in this. This Jira turns out to be a duplicate for YARN-6234, where people are still debating whether we should create a new pipeline or reuse it for later attempts in the same node.
No problem, if you decide to go for this option, please consider the potential issues above.
Closing this as it's a duplicate of YARN-6234. [~botong]/[~curino], please continue the discussion there.
